citation.description: audit_trail() aggregator builds and returns the AuditTrail dict with exactly the four keys; completion is None only when both attestation and sidecar are None. tests/unit/test_audit_read.py lines 57-105 and 177-187 assert the exact key set, newest-first ordering, and bare-ticket empty/None behavior.

criteria.criterion: `audit_trail(ticket_id)` returns a dict with EXACTLY the keys `ticket`, `plan_reviews`, `completion`, `code_reviews`; `plan_reviews` and `code_reviews` are lists (newest-first, `[]` when none), `completion` is `CompletionRecord | None` (`None` only when both attestation and sidecar are absent).

citation.description: test_audit_show_cli_json_shape runs `rebar audit show <ticket> --output json` via subprocess, parses JSON from stdout, and asserts set(data.keys()) == _EXPECTED_KEYS. CLI handler in src/rebar/_cli/_audit_commands.py, intercepted in src/rebar/_cli/__init__.py lines 471-474, help/audit.txt registers `audit` as a known subcommand.

criteria.criterion: `rebar audit show <ticket> --output json` prints the same structure. Oracle: a CLI subprocess test asserting on the emitted JSON's key set.

citation.description: audit_trail is registered with @mcp.tool(annotations=_ANN['READ_ONLY']) inside register_read_tools(), which is called unconditionally by build_server(). test_audit_trail_mcp_read_tool in tests/unit/test_audit_read.py (lines 162-173) sets REBAR_MCP_READONLY=1, registers the tool via the fake MCP, calls it, and asserts the AuditTrail key set.

criteria.criterion: The MCP `audit_trail` tool returns the structure and is read-only (available even with `REBAR_MCP_READONLY=1`). Oracle: a test invoking the tool handler under that env.

citation.description: All required test oracles are present with no skip/xfail marks. All implementation files exist with real (non-stub) code: src/rebar/audit/read.py, src/rebar/audit/__init__.py, src/rebar/_cli/_audit_commands.py, src/rebar/_cli/help/audit.txt, all_review_results in plan_review/sidecar.py and code_review/sidecar.py, and the MCP tool in _mcp_reads.py.

criteria.criterion: `make lint && make typecheck && make test` pass.
